metadata.title: The Arctic — the resource race, the Northern Sea Route, Greenland, and the militarization (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the resource/shipping/militarization facts, the Arctic Council paralysis, China's 'near-Arctic' posture, and the US Greenland push are FACT (treaty/govt records, news of record); motives are not imputed to states/blocs
metadata.frame: Regional block: the warming Arctic as a resource + shipping + military arena. Threads: hydrocarbons + rare earths + fisheries opening up; the Northern Sea Route (Russia) vs the Northwest Passage (Canada); militarization (Russia's rebuilt Arctic bases; Finland + Sweden joining NATO 2023-24; the US push to acquire/control Greenland, 2025); the Arctic Council paralyzed after Russia's 2022 invasion; China's self-styled 'near-Arctic state' + Polar Silk Road (BRI). Attaches to Russia, China, US_Government, NATO, Canada, BRI.

the_resources: Warming is opening the Arctic to oil/gas (a large share of undiscovered reserves), rare earths/critical minerals (Greenland), and fisheries — a new resource frontier with contested governance. grade: fact.
the_routes: Two seasonal sea lanes shorten Asia-Europe shipping: the NORTHERN SEA ROUTE along Russia's coast (Russia-controlled, China-courted) and the NORTHWEST PASSAGE through Canada's archipelago (Canada claims internal waters; the US calls it an international strait). grade: fact.
the_militarization: Security: Russia has the longest Arctic coastline + rebuilt Soviet-era bases; Finland (2023) + Sweden (2024) joined NATO, making the Arctic ~7-of-8 NATO + Russia; the US operates Pituffik (Thule) base in Greenland and in 2025 pressed to ACQUIRE/control Greenland (a Danish territory) — a major flashpoint. grade: fact.
the_governance: The Arctic Council (the 8 Arctic states' forum) was effectively PARALYZED after Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion (the 7 others suspended cooperation with the Russian chair), weakening the main governance body just as competition intensified. China styles itself a 'near-Arctic state' and pursues a Polar Silk Road (BRI) via investment + research stations. grade: fact.
the_honest_reading: The Arctic is a resource + shipping + military arena heating up literally and geopolitically: opening hydrocarbons/minerals/fisheries, the Russia-controlled NSR vs Canada's Northwest Passage, a near-fully-NATO Arctic facing Russia, China's 'near-Arctic'/Polar-Silk-Road entry, and the US's 2025 Greenland push — all while the Arctic Council sits paralyzed. Facts are dated; no unitary national 'mind' is imputed. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
